required_sections.detail: Required sections 3 (Learning Objectives), 4 (Introduction), 5 (Core Concepts), 10 (Key Takeaways) all present and non-trivial. Section 5 well over 100 words (8 subsections 5.1-5.8); sections 3, 4, 10 each over 50 words. No placeholder strings in required sections.

citation_density.detail: Primary [1] (realpython.com/python-variables) cited ~10x across 5.1, 5.2, 5.8, 6.2, 6.3, 7, 10. Supporting [2] (PEP 8) cited 5x (5.5, 7, 8, 10); supporting [3] (geeksforgeeks) cited ~14x (5.3, 5.4, 5.7, 6.1, 7, 8, 10). Only markers [1][2][3] appear and all resolve to the 3 declared resources - no fabricated/unknown markers. Key factual assertions in Core Concepts (sec 5), Implementation (sec 6), and the new Real-World Patterns (sec 7) carry [N] well above the 60% strict threshold.

sequence_drift.detail: All taught technical terms map to 1.2 concepts_introduced (variables/assignment operator =, reassignment, identifiers/snake_case, reserved keywords/case sensitivity, int/float/str/bool, type(), dynamic typing) or prior 1.1 concepts (Colab, cells/run, runtime, print()). 'bool' held at 'two values of type bool' only - no truthiness. New Section 7 introduces no forward concepts: reassignment, snake_case vs camelCase (PEP 8), keyword/built-in shadowing, case sensitivity, dynamic typing - all 1.2. No 1.3 operators/comparison/logical/truthiness taught (sec 5.6 explicitly defers operations to 'the next topic'; incidental '+' in reassignment examples illustrates a 1.2 concept, not taught as the operators topic). No 1.4 conversion int()/str()/float()/bool()-as-conversion, f-strings, type hints, match-case, or string indexing/concat. NameError/SyntaxError appear only as surfaced error messages, not as taught error-handling.

length_sanity.detail: Word count ~3310 against standard profile range 3000-4400 - within band. Re-check after prior length-fail revision (was ~1490) confirms recovery; new Section 7 (Real-World Patterns / Common Pitfalls) plus expansion of Core Concepts and Implementation brings the corpus to standard depth.

verdict: PASS - re-check after length-fail revision. All four checks pass: required_sections complete and non-trivial; citation_density strict/web_search met (only valid [1][2][3] markers, primary [1] cited ~10x, no fabrications); sequence_drift clean (no forward refs, bool held at value-only, new Section 7 introduces no forward concepts); length_sanity ~3310 words within standard band 3000-4400.
